problem_text: For the convergent alternating series sum_{n=1}^infinity (-1)^(n+1)/n^3, use the Alternating Series Remainder bound |R_N| <= b_(N+1) to find the smallest number of terms N needed so the partial sum approximates the total with error less than 0.01.

content.label: Leibniz conditions hold
content.applies_when: the series meets the Alternating Series Test conditions, so the remainder bound |R_N| <= b_(N+1) applies.

content.label: the error after N terms is at most the first omitted term: |R_N| <= b_(N+1) = 1/(N+1)^3
content.action: the error after N terms is at most the first omitted term: |R_N| <= b_(N+1) = 1/(N+1)^3
content.purpose: express the truncation error through the remainder bound

content.label: require 1/(N+1)^3 < 0.01, i.e. (N+1)^3 > 100
content.purpose: turn the tolerance into an inequality in N

content.label: since 4^3 = 64 < 100 and 5^3 = 125 > 100, we need N + 1 >= 5, so the smallest number of terms is N = 4
content.action: since 4^3 = 64 < 100 and 5^3 = 125 > 100, we need N + 1 >= 5, so the smallest number of terms is N = 4
content.purpose: produce the smallest N meeting the tolerance
